spec_match.rationale: The demo correctly implements and showcases standard CSS text-fit, min-font-size, and max-font-size properties.

interactive.rationale: The horizontal resize shell and container width range slider provide excellent real-time interactive controls to see text scaling in action.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index blurb states that the demo compares text-fit with the 'old JavaScript approach', but the demo only compares it with fixed CSS font size.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo only shows static placeholder texts ('Hello World' and 'Fill Me') and lacks controls to test longer text, line wrapping, or extreme bounds.

references.rationale: The see also section properly links to the chromestatus.com entry for feature 5104141688635392.

design_system.rationale: The page conforms to the design system, utilizing the specified CSS variables and clean typographic layout.

openQuestions.title: Add simulated JavaScript fitText comparison column
openQuestions.detail: The index page blurb claims this demo compares text-fit with the 'old JavaScript approach'. To satisfy this, we should add a fourth column that performs a JS resize-event loop (similar to fitText.js) to dynamically update font-size, which would highlight the latency and layout thrashing differences compared to native CSS.

openQuestions.title: Allow user text input to test length and wrapping edge cases
openQuestions.detail: Currently the demo uses fixed text. Adding a simple text input box so users can type their own strings would help them observe how text-fit handles extreme line lengths, spaces, and potential overflow when the minimum font-size limit is reached.

summary: A highly interactive and well-structured demo that successfully illustrates the core behavior of CSS text-fit using resizable containers and comparison columns. It can be improved by adding the promised JavaScript fallback comparison and user-editable text inputs to stress-test edge cases.
